utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all 5 corresponding sections. Check full specification per item: Each of the first three coverages specifies type (physical/mental health; home/contents; travel) and includes costs. Additional_services lists services with details and costs. Total_budget provides total monthly cost and a breakdown. All items are present and include the required details (coverage type and costs; total with breakdown).

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: We need to map each item named in the final coverage plan to the corresponding item name in the ground-truth ratings and provide the exact rating number. The final plan includes: Health: “LifeFirst Standard Bundle” (not present verbatim). The closest ground-truth items are LifeFirst Standard Plan for physical health (rating 9) and LifeFirst Standard Plan for mental health (rating 4). Since the plan labels a single bundled health coverage named ‘LifeFirst Standard Bundle’ and there is no exact match, we map to the LifeFirst Standard Plan under primary_health_coverage.physical_health, as it’s the core health plan referenced in the plan description (comprehensive physical and mental health with telemedicine and gym reimbursement aligns with LifeFirst’s features). Property: “LifeFirst Standard Coverage for Home Content” matches property_protection.home_content “LifeFirst Standard Coverage” (rating 9). Travel: “InsurePlus Standard Plan” under travel matches mobility_coverage.travel_insurance “InsurePlus Standard Plan” (rating 10). Additional services: “Professional Liability Insurance” and “Cybersecurity Insurance” do not appear in the ground-truth ratings lists, so they cannot be mapped and should be omitted from RATINGS. Therefore, we return ratings for: LifeFirst Standard Plan (health), LifeFirst Standard Coverage (home content), and InsurePlus Standard Plan (travel).
